retry_reason: The get operation never fails on a non-NULL input; the only NULL case is when the input is NULL, which is a no-op and not a failure to retry.
contract_summary: acpi_dev_get is a get-wrapper that increments the reference count of an acpi_device's underlying device (via get_device). The function returns a pointer to the same device with an elevated reference, or NULL if the input is NULL. The caller must release this reference by calling acpi_dev_put (or put_device) when done. The wrapper safely handles NULL input, returning NULL without any side effect. No locking or RCU requirements are needed beyond the standard device model rules.

check.reason: The get_device operation increments the refcount on &adev->dev, which is the device embedded in adev. The access path adev.dev.kobj.kref.refcount matches the callee's type chain (acpi_device->device->kobject->kref->refcount_t). The critical variable is the input parameter adev, and the returned pointer is the same. The segment count (5) matches the type chain count (5).
